key_insight: Q-READY proposes a model-based workflow to predict whether a hybrid quantum-classical application is feasible before implementation, using system-level models that account for hardware constraints.
engineering_relevance: The framework helps engineers compare candidate quantum solutions early, reducing costly trial-and-error under qubit, noise, and connectivity limits.
method: Requirements modeling to capture system goals and constraints
Problem formulation and workflow design with backend-aware abstractions
Simulation-based feasibility assessment using traceable MBSE/MDE models
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an illustrative credit-portfolio capital-assessment example showing how requirements, strategy, workflow, backend assumptions, and feasibility evidence are linked for comparison.
watch_item: The approach is still envisioned rather than fully validated, so its practical value will depend on implementation maturity, benchmark quality, and backend fidelity.
paper_title: Q-READY: Predictive Feasibility Assessment for Hybrid Quantum-Classical Applications
